questions.question: Which concept of health emphasizes that health is a fundamental human right and that the state has a responsibility for the health of its people?
questions.explanation: Option E is correct because the philosophical concept moves beyond medical and social definitions to view health as a fundamental right and a goal of socio-economic development. Option A is incorrect as it views health only as the absence of disease. Option B is incorrect as it focuses on the man-environment equilibrium. Option C is incorrect as it focuses on social and psychological factors. Option D is incorrect as it synthesizes all dimensions but doesn't specifically focus on the "right to health" framework.

questions.question: A 100-bed district hospital had 3,000 admissions in a year. The total number of inpatient days for the year was 25,000. The hospital administrator wants to know the "Bed Turnover Interval" (the average time a bed remains empty between patients). Which of the following is the correct calculation?
questions.explanation: Option A is correct. Bed Turnover Interval = (Total available bed days - Actual inpatient days) / Number of discharges (or admissions). Total available days = 100 beds * 365 days = 36,500. So, (36,500 - 25,000) / 3,000. Option B is incorrect as it calculates the Average Length of Stay (ALOS). Option C is incorrect as it calculates the average days each bed was occupied. Option D is incorrect as it calculates the Turnover Ratio (patients per bed). Option E is incorrect as it calculates the total empty bed days per bed, not per patient.

questions.question: During a community survey, residents are asked to rate their own health status on a scale from "Excellent" to "Poor." This type of data collection represents which category of health indicators?
questions.explanation: Option C is correct because subjective indicators (also known as perceived health indicators) rely on the individual's own assessment and reporting of their health and well-being. Option A is incorrect because objective indicators are measurable by an external observer using standardized tools (e.g., blood pressure). Option B is incorrect because while it relates to health, morbidity indicators usually track diagnosed illness or hospitalizations. Option D is incorrect as it refers to government resource allocation and political commitment. Option E is incorrect as social indicators usually measure external factors like housing, literacy, or income.

questions.question: Which component is classified under the "macro-environment" of an individual's environmental dimension of health?
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the macro-environment refers to the external environment shared by the community, such as air, water, and large-scale sanitation. Option B is incorrect as it is a personal behavior. Option C is incorrect as it is part of the micro-environment (immediate domestic surroundings). Option D is incorrect as it is a personal protective measure within the micro-environment. Option E is incorrect as it is a personal hygiene behavior.

options.A: Air pollution levels in the city
options.D: Use of a mosquito net at home

questions.question: A district health report indicates that out of 5,000 total deaths recorded in a population last year, 1,000 were due to complications of Diabetes Mellitus. Which of the following correctly represents the proportional mortality rate for Diabetes in this population?
questions.explanation: Option D is correct because the Proportional Mortality Rate is calculated by dividing the number of deaths from a specific cause by the total number of deaths from all causes in the same period, then multiplying by 100. In this scenario, (1,000 deaths from Diabetes / 5,000 total deaths) × 100 = 20%. Option A is incorrect because 5% would imply only 250 deaths out of 5,000 were due to the specific cause. Option B is incorrect because 10% would require the total deaths to be 10,000 or the specific deaths to be 500. Option C is incorrect because 15% would mean 750 deaths were caused by Diabetes. Option E is incorrect because 25% would indicate that 1,250 deaths were due to Diabetes.
